beginner.question: How do you style an input field in CSS?
beginner.answer: Use input { border, padding, font-size, background-color } to style input fields.

beginner.question: How do you style a select dropdown?
beginner.answer: Use select { appearance, padding, border, background } but note that fully customizing select is limited.

beginner.question: How do you style a button in CSS?
beginner.answer: Use button or input[type=submit] with background, color, padding, border, border-radius, and cursor: pointer.

beginner.question: How do you remove the default border on inputs?
beginner.answer: Set border: none or border: 0 to remove the default input border.

beginner.question: How do you add padding to form elements?
beginner.answer: Use padding on input, select, textarea, and button elements for better spacing.

beginner.question: What does the appearance property do?
beginner.answer: appearance: none removes the default OS-level styling from form elements, allowing full custom styling.

beginner.question: How do you change the cursor to pointer on buttons?
beginner.answer: Use cursor: pointer on button or input[type=submit].

beginner.question: How do you style placeholder text?
beginner.answer: Use ::placeholder pseudo-element to style the placeholder color, font, and size.

beginner.question: How do you create rounded inputs?
beginner.answer: Use border-radius: 4px or any value on input elements.

beginner.question: How do you set the width of a form input?
beginner.answer: Use width: 100% for full-width inputs or set a fixed pixel/percentage value.

intermediate.question: How do you create a full-width form layout?
intermediate.answer: Use display: block on labels and inputs, or use grid/flexbox to align form fields.

intermediate.question: How do you style input focus states?
intermediate.answer: Use input:focus { outline: none; border-color: blue; box-shadow: ... } to style focused inputs.

intermediate.question: How do you style checkboxes and radio buttons?
intermediate.answer: Hide the native input and use ::before/::after on the label to create custom styled controls.

intermediate.question: How do you create a floating label effect?
intermediate.answer: Use the placeholder-shown pseudo-class combined with adjacent sibling selectors to move labels on focus.

intermediate.question: How do you style form validation states?
intermediate.answer: Use :valid and :invalid pseudo-classes to style inputs green for valid or red for invalid.

intermediate.question: How do you style disabled form elements?
intermediate.answer: Use :disabled pseudo-class with reduced opacity, gray background, and cursor: not-allowed.

intermediate.question: How do you create an inline form layout?
intermediate.answer: Use display: flex on the form container with flex-direction: row for side-by-side fields.

intermediate.question: How do you style textarea elements?
intermediate.answer: Use textarea { resize: vertical; min-height: 100px; padding: ... } for styled text areas.

intermediate.question: How do you group form fields visually?
intermediate.answer: Use <fieldset> and <legend> elements with CSS styling to group related form fields.

intermediate.question: How do you add icons inside input fields?
intermediate.answer: Use position: relative on the input container and an absolute positioned icon overlay, with left padding on the input.

advanced.question: How do you build a custom select dropdown that works cross-browser?
advanced.answer: Use appearance: none, custom arrow via ::after or background-image, and wrap in a container for full control.

advanced.question: How do you create an accessible form with proper focus indicators?
advanced.answer: Use :focus-visible for keyboard focus rings, aria-describedby for error messages, and proper label associations.

advanced.question: How do you implement form validation styling using :user-invalid?
advanced.answer: :user-invalid applies styles only after the user has interacted with the field, unlike :invalid which applies immediately.

advanced.question: How do you handle autofill style overrides?
advanced.answer: Use :-webkit-autofill pseudo-class to override browser-autofilled input colors, often with box-shadow tricks.

advanced.question: How do you create a responsive multi-column form layout?
advanced.answer: Use CSS Grid with grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(300px, 1fr)) for responsive form columns.

advanced.question: How do you style the file input button?
advanced.answer: Use ::file-selector-button pseudo-element to style the button inside <input type='file'>.

advanced.question: How do you implement a progress bar for form completion?
advanced.answer: Use a combination of HTML progress element or div with width percentage controlled by CSS or JavaScript.

advanced.question: How do you use CSS custom properties for form theming?
advanced.answer: Define --input-bg, --input-border, --focus-color custom properties and apply them across all form elements.

advanced.question: How do you handle date and color input styling across browsers?
advanced.answer: Date and color inputs have limited styling support; use appearance: none for some control, but consider custom JS solutions for consistency.

advanced.question: How do you create a CSS-only toggle switch?
advanced.answer: Hide the checkbox input, use a label with ::before (track) and ::after (thumb), and toggle based on :checked state.
